video_title: Top 10 Craziest Breakfasts in #DDD Video History with Guy Fieri | Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

restaurants.name: Catalina's 2
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Columbus, Ohio. All right, Hunter, what do you feel like eating today? You know, Dad, I'm feeling some breakfast food. I know you're not a fan of that, so we can do something else. All right, don't worry, don't worry. I'm taking you to a place that does breakfast and lunch. OK. And they're doing something that I used to eat in my childhood, but they're just doing it a little bit different. Different good or different bad? Oh, different great. I mean, when their motto is peace, love, and pancake balls, you know we got to check it out. This is Catalina's 2.

dishes.description: Pancake balls made with unbleached and corn flour, local milk, and cage-free Amish eggs, cooked in clarified butter, filled with homemade dulce de leche, and served with powdered sugar, sweet and spicy bacon, and local maple syrup.
dishes.guy_response: Outstanding. One, this is not an April Skeever. I told you. Pancake ball. It's kind of glazed over the top. You got a little bit of the hard texture on the top. Right. But you get it all the way around. Then the little Dolce de Leche inside. Not too sweet. And then you dip into the maple syrup. Local. Local. Yeah. And then you refresh your palate with spicy, fatty bacon. It's awesome. I don't mind. Very crushable. The sweet and spicy bacon, really flavorful. Salty, sweet, and texture for me. It's my jam.

dishes.description: A pancake ball filled with sweet apple butter.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, that's good. That is money. That is an apple pie pancake right there. That is delicious. Fantastic.

dishes.dish_name: Everything but the Chicken Sink Sandwich
dishes.description: Southern fried chicken brined in buttermilk and Nashville hot spice, double-dredged and fried, served with French toast made from brioche, spicy bacon, cheddar cheese, powdered sugar, and local maple syrup.
dishes.guy_response: Oh. Wow. The bomb fried chicken. Wow. Juicy. Great crust. That's fried chicken to write home about. This is breakfast. Meat's fried chicken. Meat's chicken and waffles. Meat's spicy. I mean, almost turning into a dance move. Everything but the chicken sink. That's what you should call it.

restaurants.name: From Scratch
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm just like you. I like to go on road trips with my buddies, check out funky joints, eat great food. So, here I am in Carmel, California to check out a joint where I hear they're scratch making everything, and the biscuits are the bomb. This is From Scratch.

dishes.dish_name: Extreme Double Sausage, Biscuits, and Gravy
dishes.description: Cheddar biscuits topped with a sausage patty and a creamy sausage gravy infused with sambal.
dishes.guy_response: The cheddar in the biscuit is bananas. Then on top of it, the sausage patty, but you know what? It's that sambal in that sausage patty that just gives it a little bit of that extra heat. Yep. The gravy's creamy. And it's not dry and gritty. It's thick and pretty. You have license to use extreme, man. That's pretty out of bounds. I gotta tell you, it's amazing.

restaurants.name: The Cast Iron Kitchen
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in Wilmington, North Carolina in the Porter's Neck area, about a mile and a half from the beach. You won't remember that. What you will remember. Is that there's this little mom and pop joint where they're serving up some North Carolina favorites with their own funk and their own twist. This is the Cast Iron Kitchen.

dishes.description: A buttermilk biscuit topped with country sausage gravy, applewood smoked bacon, fried chicken, and pepper jack and mustard cheese.
dishes.guy_response: So the gravy's not really big and heavy. It's a lot lighter than I thought it would be. When you come to North Carolina, and you think that you're going to have a gravy, bacon, chicken, cheese, biscuit meal, that's the kind of dish you fall asleep in. That's right.

restaurants.name: Dulce Dough
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here on St. Simons Island, Georgia. I've been here a bunch of times. You talk about a great destination for food, cool place to come and vacate with the family. But today is not like a normal visit. No, today is a first. You see, I'm not a big dessert person. So today, when I say it's a first, I'm going to a place that all they do is make desserts. But I hear it's like taking a trip right into grandma's kitchen. This is Dulce Dough.

dishes.description: Brioche dough fried and glazed with a rum syrup, filled with caramelized pineapple jam and a dulce pastry cream made with roasted white chocolate.
dishes.guy_response: Really good. The caramelization of the rum, spot on. The dulce is great. And I love this little caramel sugar chocolate that goes through it. But it's the pineapple that kind of ties everything together. It gives you a little acidity. Thank you. Delicious.

dishes.description: Brioche dough rolled with cinnamon sugar, baked on a bed of brown sugar caramel and pecans, served with cold butter.
dishes.guy_response: The salt on the butter. It's kind of like ice cream. Salty ice cream. I am going to be so loaded up on sugar, I'm going to push the car down the street here. That was great. Pecan sticky bun. It's sticky and not too sweet. I just really like it.

restaurants.name: Commonwealth
restaurants.guy_intro: I'm here in Modesto, California, about an hour south of Sacramento. You're going to love this story. Two high school buddies that live in separate parts of the state decide to move back to Modesto because it's a great place to raise their families. And they also decide, hey, why don't we open up a craft beer joint and make scratch-made food? You got to love the American dream. This is commonwealth.

dishes.description: A waffle made with buttermilk and vanilla, cut in half and filled with cheese, home fries, ham cooked in maple mustard butter, and topped with a fried egg.
dishes.guy_response: Yeah. Mm-hmm. Oh, boy. The waffle should be illegal. It actually is. You should have to weigh in before you can eat that thing. Nice job, guys. Thank you very much.

restaurants.name: Charlie Parker's Diner
restaurants.guy_intro: So now I'm going to take you to a joint that was an airplane hangar in World War II. Then it became a town hall. Well, then a plumbing shop. And, of course, it evolved into a restaurant. Did I mention it's a Quonset hut? Eating good food in a Quonset hut. Well, it works for me. Quonset huts were the big silver buildings the Army put up all over the country in the 40s and 50s. Half a century later, in Springfield, Illinois, this one's still standing as Charlie Parker's diner.

dishes.description: A massive dish featuring two pieces of toast, a ham steak, a burger patty, three scrambled eggs, sausage, bacon, and potatoes, all smothered in a homemade cheese sauce.
dishes.guy_response: This is the whole horseshoe sandwich. Happy horseshoeing. Yes. That should at least cover you until lunch. I'll still be eating it until lunch.

dishes.description: A giant 16-inch pancake made with 18 ounces of batter.
dishes.guy_response: You got the best of me.

restaurants.name: The Skirted Effort
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the center of it all, right downtown in Colorado Springs, Colorado, right across from Acacia Park. Now, I'm here to check out a funky, funky burger joint owned by a local family that has a name that you just won't forget. This is the Skirted Effort.

dishes.description: A one-third pound grass-fed beef patty on a focaccia bun, topped with cream cheese, peach wood bacon, and Bloody Mary sauce. Served with fries seasoned with brown sugar, kosher salt, and cayenne.
dishes.guy_response: Oh, my God. That is so good. The burger's dynamite. With an attitude of keeping it local. You can eat it with a clear conscience, knowing that the cattle is well-treated. Is this some of your beef? Yes. That is dynamite. Our own beef, like, amazes me every time I eat it. But when I come here, it's like, oh, that's dynamite. That's dynamite. That's dynamite. That's dynamite. All those little points make this a great burger joint.

restaurants.name: Magnolia Pancake House
restaurants.guy_intro: This is a made-for-culinary TV movie. Hotel restaurant vet, been cooking since he was 13 years old, returns to San Antonio, Texas, to open the mega breakfast and lunch. serving over 20,000 scratch meals a month. This is Magnolia Pancake House.

dishes.description: Brisket brined for 14 days with various spices, then slow-cooked for four hours. Cooled overnight, chopped, and mixed with a garlic-onion puree and potatoes, then pan-fried until crispy and topped with a fried egg.
dishes.guy_response: There's an orchestra of flavor. It is the way it's cooked. It's supposed to be done. It's been this lifelong quest for me.

restaurants.name: Bagelsaurus
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm here in the Cambridge-Somerville area right outside of Boston to check out a joint where this chef was gonna be a schoolteacher. Well, she kinda changed gears and decided to open a restaurant offering up one of my favorite things. It's something that takes so much time, so much energy, and it's really a labor of love. It's bagels. This is Bagelsaurus.

dishes.dish_name: BLT with Spicy Pepper Cream Cheese on an Onion Bagel
dishes.description: A boiled and baked onion bagel, spread with spicy pepper cream cheese, layered with oven-roasted tomatoes, red leaf lettuce, and bacon.
dishes.guy_response: The only thing that I don't like about this bagel is that one quarter of it's already gone. As a bagel, it's of the best we've ever had on the show. Light, tender, great chew. Bacon's great. But these oven roasted plum tomatoes, the acid of that cut with the fat and the spiciness of the jalapenos and poblanos in the cream cheese, you're a dangerous woman. I'm not kidding you.

restaurants.name: Full Belly Deli
restaurants.guy_intro: So I'm just like you guys. I mean, not just for Triple D, but for me and for my family. I'm always looking for great restaurants. I'm always on the hunt. So a few years ago, I was in Truckee, California. Everybody tells you about this place. You gotta get the sandwich here. So I did, and it was dynamite. Well, now I'm in Reno, Nevada, and I find out these dudes have got a second location. I gotta check it out. This is Full Belly Deli.

dishes.description: A burrito filled with homemade chorizo sausage gravy, light and flaky biscuits, cheddar cheese, and crispy hash browns, then pressed.
dishes.guy_response: As scared of it as I was. For some reason, the way you've made it, the way you've seasoned it, it really is not overly heavy. It's delicious. The biscuit is light and flaky in there. The crunch of the hash brown inside is really nice. But the kicker for me is the gravy, bro. But I just have a bowl of gravy. This is a ski-bum delight. 101. Well done, man.
